evals.name: custom-directive-focus
evals.prompt: I want a v-focus directive that focuses an input when it mounts

assertions.text: Defines a local directive in <script setup> as const vFocus = { mounted: (el) => el.focus() }, used as v-focus in the template, and notes that the mounted DOM hook runs client-side only under SSR

evals.name: directive-arg-modifiers
evals.prompt: Write a custom directive that sets an element's color from its value, supports an arg to target background instead, and a .bold modifier

assertions.text: Reads binding.value, binding.arg and binding.modifiers, applies logic in both mounted and updated hooks (or function shorthand), JavaScript only with no code comments

evals.name: plugin-install-provide
evals.prompt: How do I author a small Vue plugin that registers a global directive and provides app-level config, then wire it into the app?

assertions.text: Defines an install(app, options) plugin that calls app.directive(...) and app.provide(...), exports a factory placed in src/plugins/, wires it via app.use(plugin, options) in main.js, prefers provide/inject over app.config.globalProperties, and guards browser APIs for SSR

evals.name: negative-stateful-logic-to-composable
evals.prompt: I need to share the same stateful logic (a counter with increment/reset) across several components. Should I write a custom directive or a plugin for this?

assertions.text: Routes to vue3-composition: explains that stateful logic reuse is the job of a composable (useXxx), not a directive or plugin, since composables are the primary reuse mechanism in this project
